methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Atomic Industries is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Aaron Slodov and Lou Young Jr., having raised $25M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of nine private peers and one public comparable in the robotics and manufacturing sector, including companies such as 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and Collaborative Robotics at Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M raised as a proxy for current valuation, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression, yielding $50M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B. The median valuation across these peers is $1.1B, reflecting material dispersion in the peer set driven by varying market positions and funding stages.

These methods triangulate to a low estimate of $42M, a median of $562M, and a high of $1.2B. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median outputs reflects structural uncertainty: early-stage step-up multiples are conservative, while peer medians are pulled upward by later-stage Series B companies and one outlier ($10B valuation). Key uncertainties include actual capital efficiency relative to peers, differentiation in product-market fit, and whether Atomic should be valued closer to earlier-stage or more mature peer comparables. A range of $50M to $1.1B appears most defensible given current data.
